title: quassia suffruticosa
intro: Quassia suffruticosa, also known as Picrasma crenata or Picrasma excelsa, is a plant species that belongs to the family Simaroubaceae and is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The medicinal properties of Quassia suffruticosa include its ability to act as a natural insecticide and to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions. The health benefits of Quassia suffruticosa include its potential to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as to act as a digestive aid. Herbal preparations of Quassia suffruticosa include teas, tinctures, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
